Interface JDACIntrospection

All Superinterfaces:
IntrospectionSkeleton<JDACIntrospection, JDACScope>
All Known Implementing Classes:
JDACIntrospectionImpl
public interface JDACIntrospection extends IntrospectionSkeleton<JDACIntrospection, JDACScope>

The JDACIntrospection type is the central element of the property system. Its purpose is to expose the property and event system to the user.

Each JDACIntrospection instance is bound to a Scope, allowing accessing the scopes' and its parents' Properties. For more information visit the documentation of Scope. Unless the root scopes' JDACIntrospection instance, each JDACIntrospection instance is child of another, inheriting its values that are combines with its own. For more information, visit the section below

Scopes access

Beside using IntrospectionSkeleton.get(SpecificProperty) directly on an introspection instance, you can sometimes utilize Java's ScopedValue API to get a properties value. For that to work, the JDACIntrospection class features multiple static methods like scopedGet(JDACProperty). Methods using this scopes system, are always prefixed with "scoped".

For further understanding, it is helpful to know how the ScopedValue API works. In short: The ScopedValue API allows data to be available in a "context" whether only by field. A context is for example the callchain in which you are, take following example:

static ScopedValue<JDACIntrospection> VAL = ScopedValue.newInstance();

void one() {
    inner();
}

void two() {
    JDACIntrospection introspection = VAL.get();
    // the call above will fail
}

void inner() {
    JDACIntrospection introspection = VAL.get();
    // use the introspection.instance()
}

void main() {
    JDACIntrospection introspection = JDACIntrospectionImpl.create(...)
                                        ...
                                        .build();

    // calls "one" with the ScopedValue "VAL" set to the introspection variable
    ScopedValue.where(VAL, introspection).run(() -> one());

    two();
}

The value for "VAL" is set for all method calls inside the lambda of run(() -> one()). This means that it's accessible inside "one", "inner" and any subsequent method call, but not inside two because it is called outside of run().

The JDACIntrospection class implements the above logic natively, covering up the underlying ScopedValue. Instead of ScopedValue.get() you can use scopedGet(JDACProperty).

Please note, that the availability of scoped access to introspection instances vary widely and is different for places inside the callchain. Places where you can use it are marked by IntrospectionAccess and additionally include all interaction controller methods. For more information take a look at the wiki.

Properties

An instance of JDACIntrospection can hold JDACPropertyProviders for all properties accessible by its Scope. An introspection instance will first compute its own value using the providers registered at it and possibly combine it with the values from the introspection instances' parent(s). For more information on how this is done, visit the documentation of SingletonPropertySkeleton, MappingPropertySkeleton and EnumerationPropertySkeleton.

After computing a value, it will be cached for the lifetime of that introspection instance. Accessing such an instance is threadsafe, and it is guaranteed to always return the same instance.

Listeners

A Listener registered on an JDACIntrospection instance is stored for the lifetime of this instance. It will be called if the event it is registered for, is either fired in this introspection instance itself or any children of it.

Introspection A = ...;
Introspection B with B is children of A

A.subscribe(FooEvent.class, _ -> System.out.println("Foo fired"));

publish FooEvent in A -> "Foo fired" printed
publish FooEvent in B -> "Foo fired" printed

// ---------------------------------------
B.subsribe(BarEvent.class, _ -> System.out.println("Bar fired"));

publish BarEvent in A -> nothing printed
publish BarEvent in B -> "Bar fired" printed

Note on Propane

Propane is the underlying library, that JDA-Commands is using for its property system. In most cases, you won't directly access propane but the wrappers around it provided by JDA-Commands like JDACIntrospection, JDACProperty etc. These are generated by propane as specializations of general types like JDACIntrospection and Property. If you debug your bot or encounter any problems, it may be helpful to visit the documentation or source code of Propane.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static boolean
    accessible()
    Whether scoped access to the introspection instance (by calling scopedGet(JDACProperty)) is possible.
    static JDACIntrospection
    accessScoped()
    Returns the introspection instance set via ScopedValue if set, else throws NoSuchElementException.
    <T> T
    get(JDACProperty<T> specific)
    Returns the value for the requested property by either retrieving it from the cache or computing it according to the class' documentation.
    static <T> T
    scopedGet(JDACProperty<T> property)
    Shorthand for accessScoped().get(property).
    Subscription<JDACIntrospection, JDACScope>
    subscribe(Listener<? extends Event<JDACScope>, JDACScope, JDACIntrospection> listener)
    Subscribes to an event with the given Listener.

    Methods inherited from interface IntrospectionSkeleton

    get, scope

  • Method Details

    • get

      <T> T get(JDACProperty<T> specific)
      Returns the value for the requested property by either retrieving it from the cache or computing it according to the class' documentation.
      Parameters:
      specific - the requested property
      Returns:
      the value of the requested property
      See Also:
      • JDACIntrospection' class documentation

    • accessible

      static boolean accessible()
      Whether scoped access to the introspection instance (by calling scopedGet(JDACProperty)) is possible.
      Returns:
      whether scoped access is possible

    • accessScoped

      static JDACIntrospection accessScoped()
      Returns the introspection instance set via ScopedValue if set, else throws NoSuchElementException.
      Returns:
      the introspection instance of this scope
      See Also:
      • ScopedValue.get()

    • scopedGet

      static <T> T scopedGet(JDACProperty<T> property)
      Shorthand for accessScoped().get(property). Throws NoSuchElementException if accessible() returns false.
      Parameters:
      property - the property to get
      Returns:
      the value for the property

    • subscribe

      Subscription<JDACIntrospection, JDACScope> subscribe(Listener<? extends Event<JDACScope>, JDACScope, JDACIntrospection> listener)
      Subscribes to an event with the given Listener. The provided listener will be stored in this instance, thus be available for the lifetime of this introspection instance.
      Specified by:
      subscribe in interface IntrospectionSkeleton<JDACIntrospection, JDACScope>
      Parameters:
      listener - the Listener to be registered
      Returns:
      a Subscription identifying the registered listener
      See Also:
      • JDACIntrospection' class documentation
      • JDACEvent
